title: Smartchime OLED v2 Message Contract
description: Full display state snapshot sent from Home Assistant to the Smartchime OLED manager via MQTT (topic: smartchime/display/oled).

active.description: Whether the OLED display is on (show) or off (hide).

contrast.description: Display contrast level. Converted to 0–255 internally.

line1.description: Status bar configuration (top 10px of the 128x32 OLED).

mode.description: Comma-separated list of status bar elements to display. At least one required. Valid values: 'clock', 'motion'.

motion.description: Motion detection state. Required when mode includes 'motion'.

active.description: Whether motion is currently detected. Displays 'now' when true.

timestamp.description: ISO 8601 timestamp of the last motion event. Used to display elapsed time (e.g., '5m', '2h'). Naive timestamps are assumed UTC.

line2.description: Content area configuration (bottom 22px of the 128x32 OLED).

mode.description: Content display mode. Currently only 'rotate' is supported.

rotate_seconds.description: Seconds between rotating to the next item.

items.description: Items to rotate through, displayed in descending priority order. Each item scrolls horizontally if wider than the display.

key.description: Unique identifier for the item. Used for logging, internal reference, and detecting item list changes across state updates.

text.description: Text to display. Scrolls horizontally if wider than 128px. Truncated at 500 characters.

priority.description: Display priority. Items are shown in descending priority order (highest first).

override.description: Override/alert system. When active, replaces line2 item rotation with a single alert message.

active.description: Whether the override is active. When true, override.text replaces line2 rotation.

text.description: Alert message to display. Scrolls horizontally if wider than 128px.

expires_at.description: ISO 8601 timestamp when the override auto-expires and rotation resumes. Null means no auto-expiry (requires explicit override.active=false to clear). Naive timestamps are assumed UTC.
